question.content: "Could you help me with a few tasks? First, I'd like to book a hotel room at the 'Hilton' in 'Los Angeles, CA' from '2022-05-01' to '2022-05-10' and I need '2' rooms. Second, I'm curious about the time difference between 'New York, NY' and 'Los Angeles, CA'. Third, I've been trying to keep track of my health and I'd like to calculate my Body Mass Index (BMI). I weigh '75' kilograms and I'm '180' centimeters tall, and I'd like to use the 'metric' system. Lastly, I've written a piece of text in 'English' and I'd like to perform a sentiment analysis on it. The text is 'I had a wonderful day at the beach. The weather was perfect and I enjoyed a delicious ice cream.' Can you assist me with these?"

functions.name: calculate_bmi
functions.description: Calculate the Body Mass Index (BMI) for a person based on their weight and height.

weight.description: The weight of the person in kilograms.

height.description: The height of the person in centimeters.

system.description: The system of units to be used, 'metric' or 'imperial'. Default is 'metric'.

functions.name: hotel_booking
functions.description: Books a hotel room for a specific date range.

location.description: The city and state, e.g. New York, NY.

start_date.description: The start date of the reservation. Use format 'YYYY-MM-DD'.

end_date.description: The end date of the reservation. Use format 'YYYY-MM-DD'.

rooms.description: The number of rooms to reserve.

functions.name: sentiment_analysis
functions.description: Perform sentiment analysis on a given piece of text.

text.description: The text on which to perform sentiment analysis.

language.description: The language in which the text is written.

functions.name: get_time_difference
functions.description: Get the time difference between two places.

place1.description: The first place for time difference.

place2.description: The second place for time difference.

text: I had a wonderful day at the beach. The weather was perfect and I enjoyed a delicious ice cream.

model_raw_output:
<tool_call>
{"name": "hotel_booking", "arguments": {"hotel_name": "Hilton", "location": "Los Angeles, CA", "start_date": "2022-05-01", "end_date": "2022-05-10", "rooms": 2}}
<tool_call>
{"name": "get_time_difference", "arguments": {"place1": "New York, NY", "place2": "Los Angeles, CA"}}
<tool_call>
{"name": "calculate_bmi", "arguments": {"weight": 75, "height": 180, "system": "metric"}}
<tool_call>
{"name": "sentiment_analysis", "arguments": {"text": "I had a wonderful day at the beach. The weather was perfect and I enjoyed a delicious ice cream.", "language": "English"}}

question.content:
You are a teacher preparing a probability lesson for your students. You have a deck of 52 playing cards and you want to explain the probability of drawing certain cards. 

1. What is the probability of drawing an Ace (4 successful outcomes) from the deck (52 total outcomes)? Please provide this as a decimal. 

2. Then, what is the probability of drawing a heart (13 successful outcomes) from the deck (52 total outcomes)? Please provide this as a decimal.

question.content: "Imagine you are a real estate investor. You bought a property 5 years ago for $500,000. The annual depreciation rate for the property is 2%. Can you calculate the current depreciated value of the property? Now, consider you had a sum of $200,000 at the same time you bought the property. If the annual inflation rate has been 3% for the past 5 years, how much would that sum be worth today? Also, suppose you took out a loan of $300,000 with an annual interest rate of 4% to help finance the property purchase. question.content: Find me a vegan recipe for brownies which prep time is under 30 minutes.

functions.name: find_recipe
functions.description: Find a recipe based on the dietary restrictions, recipe type, and time constraints.

dietary_restrictions.description: Dietary restrictions e.g. vegan, vegetarian, gluten free, dairy free.

recipe_type.description: Type of the recipe. E.g. dessert, main course, breakfast.

time.description: Time limit in minutes to prep the meal.

functions.name: science_history.get_discovery_details
functions.description: Retrieve the details of a scientific discovery based on the discovery name.

discovery.description: The name of the discovery, e.g. Gravity

method_used.description: The method used for the discovery, default value is 'default' which gives the most accepted method.

question.content: Convert 150 Euros to Canadian dollars.

functions.name: currency_conversion.convert
functions.description: Convert a value from one currency to another.

question.content: Calculate the Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) for an initial investment of $2000, final value of $3000 in a period of 4 years.

functions.name: calculate_cagr
functions.description: Calculate the Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) given an initial investment value, a final investment value, and the number of years.

period_in_years.description: The period of the investment in years.

question.content: Calculate the probability of drawing a king from a deck of cards.

functions.name: calculate_probability
functions.description: Calculate the probability of an event.

round_to.description: Number of decimal places to round the result to.

question.content: Generate a DNA sequence with 100 bases including more G (Guanine) and C (Cytosine).

functions.name: generate_DNA_sequence
functions.description: Generate a random DNA sequence with a specific length and nucleotide preference.

length.description: The length of the DNA sequence to be generated.

preferences.description: Preferred nucleotides to include more frequently in the DNA sequence.

question.content: How to obtain the detailed case information of the 'R vs Adams' legal case?

functions.name: legal_case.fetch
functions.description: Fetch detailed legal case information from database.

case_id.description: The ID of the legal case.

details.description: True if need the detail info.

question.content: You are given a dataset with various variables including 'Age', 'Income', 'Education', 'Gender', 'Marital Status', and 'Spending Score'. You want to predict 'Spending Score' based on the other variables. Could you please use the 'run_linear_regression' function to build a linear regression model using 'Age', 'Income', and 'Education' as predictor variables and 'Spending Score' as the target variable without applying standardization on the predictors? Then, could you please run the same function again but this time with standardization applied on the predictors?

functions.name: run_linear_regression
functions.description: Build a linear regression model using given predictor variables and a target variable.

predictors.description: Array containing the names of predictor variables.

standardize.description: Option to apply standardization on the predictors. Defaults to False.
